retrieved_docs.title: Retinal Detachment -- Pathophysiology
retrieved_docs.content: An RRD can occur over hours to months, depending on the location of the detachment. An inferior RRD due to an inferior atrophic retinal hole or inferior retinal dialysis in a young individual usually takes years to involve the fovea and become symptomatic. On the contrary, a superior horseshoe tear can cause rapid progression of superior RRD that involves the fovea early in an older patient with the posterior vitreous detachment (PVD) and liquefied vitreous. [31] An RD can lead to severe vision loss; without surgical intervention, RD can be a permanently blinding condition.

retrieved_docs.title: Renal Failure (Archived) -- Pearls and Other Issues
retrieved_docs.content: The Modification of Diet in Renal Disease (MDRD) formula includes age, gender, race, BUN, creatinine, and albumin. These are all important factors in measuring kidney function (GFR) and all automatically printed in lab reports.

retrieved_docs.title: Aortic Regurgitation (Archived) -- Complications -- Chronic AR
retrieved_docs.content: Initial phases of chronic AR are subclinical and may not show any signs and symptoms. [19] However, as it progresses, it affects cardiac hemodynamics and functioning. It can manifest as progressive LV systolic dysfunction, congestive heart failure, ischemic cardiomyopathy, arrhythmia, and even sudden death. The benefits of surgery outweigh risks in patients with congestive symptoms or exercise intolerance; thus, valve surgery in these patients is well justified to prevent complications. [20]

retrieved_docs.title: Posterior Urethral Valves -- Complications -- Renal Failure
retrieved_docs.content: In a study involving 274 patients receiving initial treatment within the first 90 days of life at 5 different hospitals, findings revealed that 16% required renal replacement therapy within 10 years. [114] These patients were risk-stratified utilizing the lowest creatinine levels recorded within the first year as a critical parameter for classification. [114]

retrieved_docs.title: Renal Failure (Archived) -- Etiology -- Chronic Renal Failure [4]
retrieved_docs.content: Diabetes mellitus, especially type 2 diabetes mellitus, is the most frequent cause of ESRD. Hypertension is the second most frequent cause. Glomerulonephritis Polycystic kidney diseases Renal vascular diseases Other known causes, like prolonged obstruction of the urinary tract, nephrolithiasis Vesicoureteral reflux, a condition in which urine backs up into the kidneys Recurrent kidney infections/ pyelonephritis Unknown etiology

retrieved_docs.title: Pellucid Marginal Corneal Degeneration -- Treatment / Management
retrieved_docs.content: The fitting of contact lenses in PMCD is, in general, more challenging than other ectatic diseases due to the large area of inferior protrusion and inability of the contact lens to ‘sit on the cone.’ Routine rigid gas permeable (RGP) lenses often pose decentration challenges. Large diameter RGP lenses may be particularly useful. Other contact lenses like hybrid lenses with a soft skirt, semi scleral and scleral lenses, toric lenses, and prosthetic replacement of ocular surface ecosystem (PROSE) lenses have been found to be useful. [22] [23] [24] [25]

retrieved_docs.title: Retinal Detachment -- Etiology
retrieved_docs.content: Rhegmatogenous retinal detachment, RRD (see Image. Superotemporal Rhegmatogenous Retinal Detachment), is associated with a retinal break where liquefied vitreous enters the subretinal space. This process is facilitated by traction over the retinal break that keeps the gap open, ensuring that the entry of liquefied vitreous or fluid to the subretinal space supersedes the RPE pump, which helps absorb the subretinal fluid. Risk factors for RRD include

retrieved_docs.title: End-Stage Renal Disease -- Complications -- Complications due to ESRD
retrieved_docs.content: Coronary heart disease This is a significant complication of chronic kidney disease and is the most common cause of death in this population. Patients on dialysis have a 10 to 30 times higher cardiovascular mortality risk than the general population. [82] Peripheral vascular disease [83] Hypertension Mineral and bone disorders (secondary to hyperparathyroidism, vitamin D deficiency) Hyperuricemia Metabolic acidosis Hyperphosphatemia Hypoalbuminemia Anemia Decreased libido, erectile dysfunction

retrieved_docs.title: Dental Implants -- Complications
retrieved_docs.content: The most common postoperative complication is incision line opening. The design of the removable interim prosthesis is involved, it is corrected. The patient is instructed to rinse 2-3 times daily with chlorhexidine. If granulation process extends for more than two weeks, epithelial margin trimming can be done. If implants become exposed during the healing period, no attempt should be made to cover them with tissue. Rather denture is relieved aggressively over the area with implant exposure. The mobility of the implant during healing is unusual but may occur, mostly accompanied by a radiolucent zone around the implant. Whatever may be the cause, the implant should be removed. Signs and symptoms of failure for an implant are horizontal mobility  greater than 0.5 mm, rapid progressive bone loss, pain during percussion, uncontrolled exudate, generalized radiolucency around the implant, more than one half of the bone is lost around the implant and last the implants inserted in poor position, making them useless for prosthetic support. A success rate of 85%at the end of 5 year period and 80% at the end of 10 year period are minimum criteria for success. [15] [16] [17] [18]

retrieved_docs.title: Prisms -- Clinical Significance -- Prisms in Age-Related Macular Degeneration (ARMD)
retrieved_docs.content: These are based on the principle of image relocation. Prisms are added to the glass prescription to produce image relocation to the presumed retinal locus. The effect is probably created by the facilitation of oculomotor function resulting from the reduction of fixation instability. [64]

retrieved_docs.title: Retinal Traction Detachment -- Pathophysiology -- Types of RTD
retrieved_docs.content: RTD threatening macula: The area of retinal elevation is at least 4 disc diameter, some part of whose is within the 30 degrees of the center of the macula or retinal elevation of less than 4 disc area along with one or more vitreoretinal adhesion causing elevation of the retina within 30 degrees of center of macula in the presence of new vessel or fresh vitreous hemorrhage. RTD involving macula: Vitreo-retinal traction along the arcade, disc, or along retinal traction lines extending through the fovea and causing vision loss. The rate of progression of extramacular RTD to involve the macula ranges from 14 to 15% at 1 year and 21 to 23% at the end of 2 years. [14] [15] RTD should be differentiated from the other 2 types of retinal detachments (RD), i.e., rhegmatogenous RD and exudative RD (see figures).

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 21 -- Level 1 -- Community 61
retrieved_docs.content: Hemodialysis is a specific modality of renal dialysis that functions as a parent procedure for various therapeutic and diagnostic concepts. It is directly linked to acute kidney failure, being listed as a member of the acute renal failure syndrome (الفشل الكلوي الحاد (SMQ)) and having a formal relationship with acute renal insufficiency (قصور كلوي حاد). Clinical entities that arise as direct consequences of hemodialysis include the Acute disorder of hemodialysis and the First use syndrome of dialysis; both are described as “due to” hemodialysis and are characterized by a sudden‑onset clinical course, exemplified by the dialysis disequilibrium syndrome (متلازمة اختلال غسيل الكلى). The contextual qualifier “Sudden onset (contextual qualifier) (qualifier value)” is also applied to Sudden acquired retinal degeneration, indicating that the sudden‑onset pattern is a broader descriptor of disease presentation. Hemodialysis is clinically associated with several systemic complications: chronic

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 25 -- Level 3 -- Community 1961
retrieved_docs.content: Hemodialysis is linked to a spectrum of acute and chronic complications. Acute disorder of hemodialysis and First use syndrome of dialysis both arise as direct consequences of hemodialysis and are characterized by a sudden‑onset clinical course. The dialysis‑related condition described as متلازمة اختلال غسيل الكلى (dialysis‑syndrome) is also caused by hemodialysis and shares this abrupt clinical presentation. Clinically, hemodialysis is associated with chronic hypertension, اختلال وظيفة الكلى (renal functional impairment), فقر دم صغير الكريات (microcytic anemia), and acute renal failure (قصور كلوي حاد). Hemodialysis can also precipitate low blood pressure (ضغط الدم المنخفض). In addition, Sudden acquired retinal degeneration is noted to have a sudden‑onset clinical course, illustrating that abrupt onset is a common qualifier for several severe medical events. These interrelationships underscore the importance of vigilant monitoring for both systemic and organ‑specific adverse effects in patients receiving hemodialysis.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 12 -- Level 1 -- Community 208
retrieved_docs.content: diabetes mellitus—all localized to the retina. The retina is also the primary site for retinal detachments, structural defects, vascular occlusions, and various other retinopathies. Therapeutic interventions targeting these lesions comprise destructive procedures on the posterior segment of the eye, categorized under “destruction of lesion of retina and choroid,” with radiation‑based destruction of localized retinal lesions (e.g., macular edema or tumors) as a further sub‑category. The disease داء ريفسام is likewise identified with retinal findings, underscoring the eye’s role in its manifestation. Clinical assessment employs the concept of “general clinical state” to interpret both biologic function failure and uncontrolled disease conditions; uncontrolled disease conditions are associated with diabetes mellitus with complications, highlighting the need for vigilant monitoring. Biologic function failure is linked to hepatic‑renal failure, indicating a critical organ‑system

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 24 -- Level 1 -- Community 202
retrieved_docs.content: diabetes mellitus, its specific complications (various forms of retinopathy and nephropathy), and the relationship between these complications and ocular destructive procedures is essential for comprehensive patient assessment and management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 4 -- Level 3 -- Community 2341
retrieved_docs.content: Failure (biologic function) is associated with الفَشَلٌ الكَبِدِيُّ الكُلْوِيّ, indicating that a general loss of biological function can be linked to hepatorenal failure—a severe condition in which both liver and kidney functions are compromised. Recognizing this association is important for clinicians, as it highlights the need to assess and manage systemic organ failure comprehensively in affected patients.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 10 -- Level 1 -- Community 266
retrieved_docs.content: and retina, leading to scarring, atrophy, detachment, neovascularization and visual impairment. Accurate identification of the specific choroidal and retinal pathology is essential for comprehensive evaluation, multidisciplinary management and optimal patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2602
retrieved_docs.content: Acute disorder of hemodialysis, First use syndrome of dialysis, Sudden acquired retinal degeneration, and متلازمة اختلال غسيل الكلى (dialysis dysfunction syndrome) are medical conditions that share a common clinical characteristic: a sudden‑onset course. In each of these disorders the disease manifests abruptly, indicating that their clinical course is defined by a rapid appearance of symptoms. Recognizing this sudden‑onset pattern is important for timely diagnosis and appropriate management of complications related to hemodialysis, initial dialysis use, retinal degeneration, or dialysis dysfunction.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 13 -- Level 4 -- Community 2904
retrieved_docs.content: Diabetic retinopathy (اعتِلال الشبكية السُّكَّرِيّ) is clinically associated with end‑stage renal disease (مرض كلوي في المرحلة النهائية). Both conditions represent severe microvascular complications of long‑standing diabetes mellitus, and the presence of diabetic retinopathy often signals an increased risk of progression to advanced kidney failure. Recognizing this association is essential for clinicians, as early detection and management of retinal changes can prompt more aggressive monitoring of renal function, and coordinated care can help mitigate the morbidity associated with simultaneous vision loss and the need for renal replacement therapy.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 8 -- Level 3 -- Community 2169
retrieved_docs.content: Acute disorder of hemodialysis, sudden acquired retinal degeneration, and متلازمة اختلال غسيل الكلى (dialysis dysfunction syndrome) are all described as having a clinical course characterized by sudden onset. The “sudden onset” qualifier indicates that the manifestations of these conditions develop rapidly, which is a key feature for clinicians to recognize when diagnosing and managing these disorders. Understanding that a rapid, abrupt presentation is common to these entities helps guide timely evaluation and appropriate therapeutic interventions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 24 -- Level 1 -- Community 202
retrieved_docs.content: hierarchically. “Destruction of localized lesion of retina (eg, macular edema, tumors), one or more sessions; radiation by implantation of source” is a child procedure of the broader category “Destructive procedure on posterior segment of eye,” which itself is a parent of “destruction of lesion of retina and choroid.” The procedure “destruction of lesion of retina and choroid” has a direct procedure site of the retina, confirming that these destructive ocular treatments are directed at the same anatomical location affected by the diabetic retinopathies. In summary, secondary diabetes mellitus leads to retinal and renal complications, with the retina serving as the primary site of disease manifestation and therapeutic targeting. Understanding the hierarchical classification of secondary diabetes mellitus, its specific complications (various forms of retinopathy and nephropathy), and the relationship between these complications and ocular destructive procedures is essential for

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 22 -- Level 2 -- Community 499
retrieved_docs.content: Hemodialysis is linked to a range of acute and chronic complications that often present with a sudden‑onset clinical course. Acute disorder of hemodialysis arises as a direct consequence of the dialysis procedure itself and typically follows a rapid, abrupt clinical trajectory. A similar pattern is seen with the first‑use syndrome of dialysis, another complication that occurs when patients are exposed to hemodialysis for the first time; this syndrome also manifests suddenly after initiation of treatment. Both of these entities are closely related to dialysis‑associated disequilibrium syndrome (متلازمة اختلال غسيل الكلى), which is characterized by an abrupt onset and is explicitly described as having a sudden clinical course. In addition to these acute presentations, hemodialysis is clinically associated with several chronic conditions. Patients undergoing long‑term dialysis frequently exhibit chronic hypertension, persistent impairment of kidney function (اختلال وظيفة الكلى), and microcytic anemia (فقر دم صغير الكريات). Moreover, hemodialysis can precipitate or coexist with acute renal failure (قصور كلوي حاد), underscoring the potential for severe renal compromise in this population. Beyond renal‑related sequelae, the concept of sudden onset is also applied to other ophthalmic pathology, such as sudden acquired retinal degeneration, which similarly follows an abrupt clinical course, highlighting that rapid symptom emergence is a common feature across diverse medical conditions. Overall, the interconnections among hemodialysis, its acute syndromes (acute disorder of hemodialysis, first‑use syndrome, dialysis disequilibrium syndrome), associated chronic disorders (chronic hypertension, kidney function disorder, microcytic anemia, acute renal failure), and the broader pattern of sudden‑onset clinical courses emphasize the importance of vigilant monitoring and prompt management of both immediate and long‑term complications in patients receiving hemodialysis.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 28 -- Level 2 -- Community 1148
retrieved_docs.content: valve pathologies, broader cardiac disease categories, diagnostic modalities and therapeutic interventions is essential for accurate diagnosis, optimal treatment planning and comprehensive patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 9 -- Level 1 -- Community 142
retrieved_docs.content: Secondary diabetes mellitus is a diabetes mellitus with complications and serves as a parent condition for secondary diabetes mellitus without complication, itself a subtype of diabetes mellitus without complication. Its complications include diabetic chronic kidney disease, diabetic peripheral angiopathy with gangrene, nephropathy, and ocular manifestations such as mild, moderate, and unspecified non‑proliferative retinopathy, retinal detachments, vascular occlusion, and other retinopathies; all of these have the retina as the anatomical finding site. Destructive procedures on posterior ocular segment lesions, including radiation implantation for macular edema or tumors, are categorized as lesion‑destruction interventions on the retina and choroid. Clinical assessment of uncontrolled disease condition is associated with diabetes mellitus with complications, emphasizing the need for disease control in secondary diabetes mellitus. Failure of biologic function is also associated with

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 14 -- Level 1 -- Community 208
retrieved_docs.content: radiation implantation to destroy localized retinal lesions such as macular edema or tumors—are a child procedure of the broader “destructive posterior‑segment” category and are performed directly on the retina and choroid. The retina also serves as the finding site for the disease “داء ريفسام”. Together, these relationships demonstrate how functional failure, chronic disease trajectories, and interpretive impairments intersect across cardiovascular, renal, metabolic, gastrointestinal, and neurological systems, and they highlight the multidisciplinary procedural approaches required for managing such complex clinical scenarios.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 3 -- Level 2 -- Community 579
retrieved_docs.content: Diabetes (مرض السكري) is presented as a central disease entity that encompasses a wide range of related conditions, complications, and associated diagnostic components. Several specific forms of diabetes, including DIABETES MELLITUS, TRANSIENT NEONATAL, 2 (disorder), Diabetes Mellitus, Transient Neonatal, 1, and the syndrome described as Developmental Delay, Epilepsy, and Neonatal Diabetes, are all mapped to the broader category of diabetes, indicating that these neonatal and transient presentations are considered subtypes of the same metabolic disorder. Clinically, diabetes is linked to multiple serious complications. Retinal disorder (اضطراب في الشبكية) is clinically associated with diabetes, reflecting the well‑known risk of diabetic retinopathy. Congestive heart failure (فشل قلبي احتقاني) is also clinically associated with diabetes, and the procedure of amputation of lower extremity is further linked to this cardiac condition, underscoring the systemic impact of the disease.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 29 -- Level 2 -- Community 531
retrieved_docs.content: diabetes disorders, pregnancy‑related glucose intolerance, medication contraindications, retinal and peripheral vascular complications—including various levels of lower‑extremity amputation and stump necrosis—and metabolic syndrome, all of which are linked through shared comorbidity classifications. This integrated understanding emphasizes the importance of comprehensive management strategies that address both the primary metabolic disorder and its extensive systemic manifestations.
